mission: Choose the enforceable Vecells monorepo baseline covering workspace graph law, import-boundary enforcement, primary implementation languages, generated-contract publication, and the developer workflow required for one-command local startup, contract-aware CI, and Playwright-verifiable shell delivery.

chosen_build_system.boundary_enforcement: project tags + export maps + graph parity validation
chosen_build_system.rationale: Strongest balance of graph law, deterministic local execution, and low accidental complexity.

bounded_secondary_languages: Python in tools/analysis and tools/validators only
YAML and Markdown for delivery control artifacts only
chosen_language_posture.forbidden_sprawl: No Go, JVM, or Python service may own canonical runtime truth without a later explicit ADR.

build_system_scorecard.label: pnpm + Nx
build_system_scorecard.notes: Best fit for pnpm workspaces, graph-aware tags, CI-safe local determinism, and project-level ownership rules without pushing the repo into Bazel-level ceremony.
build_system_scorecard.source_refs: phase-0-the-foundation-protocol.md#0A. Delivery skeleton and repository architecture; platform-runtime-and-release-blueprint.md#CI/CD and supply-chain pipeline contract

build_system_scorecard.label: pnpm + Turborepo
build_system_scorecard.notes: Fast task runner, but boundary enforcement would still rely on extra lint layers rather than first-class project graph rules.
build_system_scorecard.rejection_reason: Rejected because the repo law depends on graph-native tags and machine-checkable boundary policy, not only task caching.
build_system_scorecard.source_refs: phase-0-the-foundation-protocol.md#0A. Delivery skeleton and repository architecture; forensic-audit-findings.md#Finding 118 - Token export and design-contract conformance could still drift outside the published runtime tuple

build_system_scorecard.label: Bazel or equivalent graph-first system
build_system_scorecard.notes: Strong graph and provenance posture, but materially more ceremony for an agent-heavy repo that still needs fast local bootstrap and human-readable workspace rules.
build_system_scorecard.rejection_reason: Rejected because the accidental complexity cost is too high for the current Phase 0 baseline and would slow contract and shell iteration.
build_system_scorecard.source_refs: phase-0-the-foundation-protocol.md#0A. Delivery skeleton and repository architecture; platform-runtime-and-release-blueprint.md#Verification ladder contract

language_posture_scorecard.label: TypeScript everywhere
language_posture_scorecard.notes: Strongest single-language posture, but it contradicts the already-established Python analysis/validator lane in tasks 001-011.
language_posture_scorecard.rejection_reason: Rejected because the repo already carries bounded Python analysis tooling and there is no architectural benefit in force-rewriting that lane into TS.
language_posture_scorecard.source_refs: phase-0-the-foundation-protocol.md#0A. Delivery skeleton and repository architecture; platform-frontend-blueprint.md#4.1D Reference implementation shape

language_posture_scorecard.label: TypeScript-first runtime plus bounded Python tooling
language_posture_scorecard.secondary_languages: Python in tools/analysis and validators only
language_posture_scorecard.notes: Keeps all browser, gateway, service, contract, and shared truth in TypeScript while preserving the bounded Python analysis lane already proven in this repo.
language_posture_scorecard.source_refs: platform-runtime-and-release-blueprint.md#Frontend and backend integration contract; platform-frontend-blueprint.md#7.8 Verification and Playwright contract

language_posture_scorecard.label: Polyglot service family split
language_posture_scorecard.runtime_languages: TypeScript + Go / Python / JVM by service family
language_posture_scorecard.notes: Could fit a larger platform later, but it raises immediate contract impedance and hidden truth risk before the runtime baseline is even frozen.
language_posture_scorecard.rejection_reason: Rejected because it would let service families drift into language-specific contract ownership and undercut the Phase 0 shared-truth law.
language_posture_scorecard.source_refs: phase-0-the-foundation-protocol.md#0A. Delivery skeleton and repository architecture; forensic-audit-findings.md#Finding 91

enforcement_stack.purpose: Defines allowed project-to-project edges and affected-target execution.
Prevents deep imports into /src, /internal, /workers, /repositories, or /adapters subpaths.
Stops TS path aliases or relative paths from bypassing the declared workspace graph.
Makes package ownership machine-checkable in review.
Fails any owner drift, illegal edges, codegen traceability loss, or language sprawl.

risks.statement: Nx tags alone are insufficient if package export maps and dependency-parity checks drift or are skipped in CI.
risks.mitigation: Gate all merges on boundary lint, export-map checks, and validate_workspace_baseline.py.

risks.statement: Generated contract packages can create large diffs that obscure meaningful source changes.
risks.mitigation: Partition generated outputs by contract family and require source digest manifests on every generated package.

risks.statement: The conditional assistive control app could accidentally become baseline scope if its package exists without an explicit status fence.
risks.mitigation: Keep workspace_status = conditional_future_optional and fail validation if it is treated as baseline in app counts or preview boot.
